name: Khakassia (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the upper stripe; mirrors the Russian tricolour
the central stripe; mirrors the Russian tricolour
the lower stripe; mirrors the Russian tricolour
the hoist stripe, representing nature, the steppe and forests of Khakassia
the sun emblem, a traditional Khakas solar symbol

description: Three equal horizontal stripes - blue, white, red - with a green vertical stripe along the hoist bearing a traditional yellow Khakas sun symbol.
history: The current design was adopted on 25 September 2003. Khakassia first adopted a flag on 6 June 1992 with similar layout but a black sun and white-blue-red horizontal stripes; the sun was changed to yellow on 23 December 1993 and the tricolour reordered to its present form in 2003.

features.symbolism: blue-white-red horizontal tricolour drawn from the Russian national flag
the green hoist band bearing the traditional Khakas sun device
a traditional yellow Khakas sun device, ancient solar symbol of the Khakas people, set on the green hoist stripe.
